data.title: FDA testing program to boost communication with drugmakers during reviews
data.description:
• Since October, the US FDA has been running a trial of a program that aims to have more frequent communication with pharma companies that have drug applications pending with the agency to ease the process.
  • FDA Commissioner Marty Makary said that the trial has only been with a handful of companies, but has been going well. One new element is that if a drugmaker has a question, they can send it to FDA with a turnaround time within three business days.
  • "If they can get that clarification, it can save them months of guesswork," Makary told Bloomberg in an interview. 
  • While the new program is being spearheaded by the Office of New Drugs, the commissioner is aiming to expand it to its other divisions.
  • Makary added the agency intends to hire 1,000 new scientists and inspectors over the next few months.
  • Earlier this year, HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. announced thousands of layoffs at the FDA.

data.title: NOV CEO Williams set to retire, succeeded by Bayardo
data.description:
NOV Inc. (NOV) said Wednesday that Chairman and CEO Clay Williams will retire, to be succeeded by current President and COO Jose Bayardo, who will become Chairman, President and CEO effective January 1.

Bayardo joined NOV (NOV) in 2015 as Senior VP and CFO before being promoted to President and COO earlier this year; before joining the company, he held executive roles at Continental Resources and Complete Production Services.

Williams became NOV's (NOV) Chairman and CEO in 2014 after holding a variety of other roles at the company, including CFO during 2005-12 and COO in 2012-14; before the merger of National Oilwell and Varco in 2005, he worked for Tuboscope, SCF Partners and Shell Oil.

data.title: Roger Mobley promoted to CFO of commercial bank
data.description:
• Commercial Bancgroup (CBK) on Wednesday announced the promotion of Roger Mobley to Chief Financial Officer of Commercial Bank effective November 1, 2025, succeeding Philip Metheny.
  • Philip Metheny will remain the CFO of Commercial Bancgroup (CBI).
  • Mobley joined Commercial Bank on July 1, 2024 with the acquisition of Alliance Bank & Trust Company (AB&T). Since that time, he has served as Assistant CFO for Commercial Bank and has been instrumental in the transition of the CBI from private to a public company.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: FDA accepts NDA for Nuvalent's zidesamtinib for non-small cell lung cancer
data.description:
• The US FDA has accepted an NDA from Nuvalent (NUVL) for zidesamtinib to treat non-small cell lung cancer.
  • The application was given an action date of Sept. 18, 2026.
  • A ROS1-selective inhibitor, zidesamtinib's application was supported by data from the ARROS-1 phase 1/2 trial in individuals who previously failed on a ROS1 tyrosine kinase inhibitor.
  • Nuvalent noted that the asset is "designed to remain active in tumors that have developed resistance to currently available ROS1 inhibitors, including tumors with treatment-emergent ROS1 mutations such as G2032R. In addition, zidesamtinib is designed for central nervous system penetrance to improve treatment options for patients with brain metastases, and to avoid inhibition of the structurally related tropomyosin receptor kinase (TRK) family."

data.title: Gold pares gains after release of Fed meeting minutes
data.description:
Gold prices regained a bit of lost ground from four straight days of losses but trimmed early gains after the release of minutes from the Federal Reserve's latest meeting painted a picture of members with "strongly differing views" on a December interest rate cut.

In their discussions on the outlook for monetary policy, FOMC members "expressed a range of views about the degree to which the current stance of monetary policy was restrictive," and expressed "strongly differing views" about the upcoming rate decision.

"The minutes are sort of water under the bridge," Marex analyst Edward Meir said in a note. "It's more important to see what happens in December and the Fed needs more data to decide that."

Traders now see just a 30% chance for a rate cut in December, according to the CME FedWatch tool.

Meanwhile, a stronger than expected non-farm payrolls report on Thursday could reignite speculation about further rate cuts in December, although this week’s data only covers September due to the government shutdown backlog, offering limited insight into the current economic landscape.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for November delivery closed +0.4% to $4,077.70/oz, and front-month Comex November silver (XAGUSD:CUR) settled +0.7% to $50.790/oz.

ETFs: (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Element announces pricing of private offering of senior notes
data.description:
• Element (EFN:CA) announced on Wednesday that it has agreed to sell $500M of 4.641% senior notes due  November 24, 2030, in a private offering.
  • The net proceeds from the offering are expected to be used for working capital and general corporate purposes, which may include the repayment of existing indebtedness.
  • The offering is expected to close on November 24, 2025.

data.title: Trump to unveil farm relief early next month, USDA head says
data.description:
The Trump administration is preparing to unveil its long-promised support package for struggling farmers in early December, U.S. Agriculture Secretary Brooke Rollins said Wednesday.

Officials had signaled for weeks that new assistance was coming, but the rollout was postponed during the federal shutdown. With government operations now fully funded, development of the plan has resumed, Rollins told Bloomberg News in a brief interview.

Rollins said the agency has spent the past several weeks reassessing market conditions and revising its approach. She noted that circumstances have shifted since discussions first began, and said the administration is aiming to announce the package in the first week of December. She did not disclose the size or structure of the planned assistance.

Farm communitiesm many of which strongly backed Donald Trump in the previous election, have faced severe financial pressure as overseas demand for U.S. crops has weakened and several federal farm programs have contracted during his second term. The president’s sweeping trade disputes have further strained producers, particularly after China halted U.S. soybean purchases in retaliation.

A temporary trade cease-fire between Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping has led Beijing to resume buying American soybeans. China has secured at least 10 additional cargoes in recent weeks, pushing seasonal purchases above 2 million tons and moving closer to commitments reportedly discussed in last month’s negotiations.

Despite the improvement in soybean shipments, Rollins said federal relief remains warranted, especially for crop producers who have endured years of tight margins. She said the administration intends to provide some measure of financial help as farmers prepare next year’s planting decisions, even if the program cannot fully offset recent losses, Bloomberg News reported.

data.title: Amgen's Imdelltra receives full FDA approval in small cell lung cancer
data.description:
• The US FDA has granted full approval to Amgen's (AMGN) Imdelltra (tarlatamab) for extensive stage small cell lung cancer.
  • Approval was based on results from the phase 3 DeLLphi-304 study that showed the biologic reduced the risk of death by 40% and demonstrated significant median overall survival compared to those on standard of care chemotherapy (13.6 months vs. 8.3 months).
  • Imdelltra received accelerated approval in May 2024.

data.title: Obsidian Energy announces $175M private placement of senior notes
data.description:
• Obsidian Energy (OBE:CA) entered an underwriting agreement to privately place $175M of 8.125% senior unsecured notes due December 3, 2030.
  • Closing is expected around December 3, 2025.
  • Net proceeds will redeem $80.8M of existing 11.95% notes due 2027, reduce borrowings under the $235M credit facility to about $5M, and cover transaction expenses.
  • The company has issued a conditional redemption notice for the 2027 notes pending successful completion of the new offering.
